name: Hominy-Squash Stew
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup Light olive oil or sunflower seed oil
1 tbsp Ground red chile or Paprika for milder flavor
1 lb Fresh or canned tomatoes (peeled, seeded and chopped; juice reserved)
3 cups Peeled, cubed banana squash (in 1-inch cubes)
Sour cream, optional. Shredded Muenster cheese.
recipeInstructions:
1. Drain the soaked beans, covering them generously with fresh water and bringing to a boil. Boil vigorously about 5 minutes, skim off any foam that rises to the surface, then lower the heat and simmer for 30 minutes. Add half a teaspoon of salt and continue cooking until the beans are tender (about another 30 minutes). Taste as they cook to ensure they are done as desired. Drain the beans but reserve the liquid.
2. In a small, heavy skillet, toast the cumin seeds until fragrant. Remove them to a plate so they don't burn. Add the oregano and stir for about 5 seconds, then quickly remove to a plate. Combine with the cinnamon stick and cloves, and grind to a powder in an electric spice mill.
3. Heat oil in a wide skillet and sauté the onion briskly over high heat for 1 minute. Lower the heat to medium and add the garlic, ground spice mixture, ground red chile or paprika, and salt to taste. Stir to combine.
4. Add half the reserved bean broth and cook, stirring occasionally, until the onions soften. Add the tomatoes and their juice, enough bean broth to cover, squash, and continue simmering until the squash is partially cooked (about 20 minutes).
5. Stir in the hominy, beans, and diced chiles. Add more broth as needed and continue cooking until the squash is tender. Taste and check seasonings.
6. Serve garnished with cilantro and sour cream or shredded cheese.
